Name: Land Agent
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 vegetable' Action space, also take 1 grain. When you play this card, take 1 vegetable from the supply.

Name: Academic
Card Text: This card counts as 2 occupations for Minor Improvements and when scoring the 'Reeve' occupation card.

Name: Master Baker
Card Text: Whenever another player bakes, you can bake if you have a Baking improvement. If you take a Bake action yourself (not using this card), take 1 additional food.

Name: Baker
Card Text: During each Harvest, you may Bake bread at the start of the Feeding phase if you have a Baking improvement. When you play this card, you may Bake bread as an additional action.

Name: Architect
Card Text: Once during the game, at any time after your home reaches at least 5 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Berry Picker
Card Text: Whenever you use a family member's action to take wood, take an additional 1 food.

Name: Mendicant
Card Text: At the end of the game, you can discard up to 2 Begging cards without losing points for them.

Name: Master Brewer
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Master Brewer can convert up to 1 grain to 3 food.

Name: Bread Seller
Card Text: Take 1 food from the supply for each bread that is baked whenever any player (including you) bakes bread.

Name: Brush Maker
Card Text: Whenever you convert wild boar to food, place them on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/3/4 slaughtered wild boar.

Name: Thatcher
Card Text: Pay 1 reeds less to build each room, each renovation, and each of the Water Mill, Half-timbered house, Chicken coop, Holiday home, Mansion and Corn storehouse.

Name: Turner
Card Text: You can use the Turner at any time to convert any number of wood to 1 food each.

Name: Head of the Family
Card Text: You can use any Building or Family growth Action space, even if another player has already placed a person on it.

Name: Farmer
Card Text: The next time you build fences, take 1 wild boar. Each time you build at least 1 fence after that, take 1 cattle marker.

Name: Fisherman
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, you can take twice as many food as are on the space. You then give 1 food each to the owners of the Fishing Rod, Raft, Canoe, Fish Trap and Landing Net.

Name: Meat-seller
Card Text: If you have an Oven, you can change your animals into food at any time. Take 2 food for each sheep, 3 for each wild boar and 4 for each cattle marker.

Name: Field Warden
Card Text: You can use the 'Take 1 vegetable', 'Plough fields' and 'Plough fields and Sow' actions even if another player has placed a person on the space.

Name: Master Forester
Card Text: Include the '2 Wood' Action card from the 3-player game as an additional forest. At the start of each round, place 2 wood on the card. Any player who uses this action must pay you 2 food.

Name: Yeoman Farmer
Card Text: At the end of the game, you only lose points in the 'Unused spaces' category and for Begging cards.

Name: Undergardener
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Day Labourer' Action space, also take 1 vegetable.

Name: Conjurer
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, take 1 additional grain.

Name: Greengrocer
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 Grain' Action space, also take 1 vegetable.

Name: Storyteller
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, you can leave 1 food on the space and take 1 vegetable instead.

Name: Estate Manager
Card Text: At the end of a 3/4/5 player game, if no player has more animals of any type than you, you receive 2/3/4 bonus points.

Name: Dock Worker
Card Text: At any time, you can use the Dock Worker to convert 3 wood to either 1 clay, 1 reeds or 1 stone, or to convert 2 clay, 2 reeds or 2 stone to one other resource.

Name: Chief
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your Stone house. (In total, you receive 3 points per room instead of 2.)

Name: Chief's Daughter
Card Text: If another player plays the 'Chief' card, you can play this card immediately at no cost. At the end of the game, you receive 3 bonus points if you have a Stone house, 1 if you have a Clay hut.
Bonus Points: 3 for stone house, 1 for clay hut

Name: Tutor
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each occupation that you play after this one.

Name: Hedge Keeper
Card Text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you can build 3 additional fences without paying any additional wood.

Name: Woodcutter
Card Text: Take 1 additional wood whenever you use a family member's action to take wood.

Name: Wooden Hut Builder
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your wooden hut.

Name: Hut Builder
Card Text: Play this card during Round 1-4 and at the start of Round 11 you can extend your hut by 1 room at no cost, as long as you have not turned it into a stone house.

Name: Merchant
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Minor Improvement' or 'Minor or Major Improvement' action, you can pay 1 food to use the action a second time.

Name: Hobby Farmer
Card Text: Take 1 vegetable when you play this card.

Name: Cook
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, only 2 of your people eat 2 food each; any others are satisfied with only 1.

Name: Charcoal Burner
Card Text: Take 1 food and 1 wood whenever any player (including you) builds a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth, Cooking Corner, Baker's Kitchen, Bakehouse or Oven.

Name: Basket Weaver
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Basket Weaver can convert up to 1 reeds to 3 food.

Name: Grocer
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 vegetable, reeds, clay, wood, vegetable, stone, grain, reeds on this card. At any time, you may buy the top item for 1 food.

Name: Clay Firer
Card Text: You can use the Clay Firer at any time to convert 2 (3) clay to 1 (2) stone.

Name: Clay Seller
Card Text: You can use the Clay Seller at any time to convert 2 clay to 1 sheep or 1 reeds, 3 clay to 1 wild boar or 1 stone and/or 4 clay to 1 cattle.

Name: Clay Deliveryman
Card Text: Place 1 clay on each of the spaces for rounds 6 to 14. At the start of these rounds, take the clay.

Name: Clay Mixer
Card Text: Whenever one of your people's actions gives you only clay, take 2 additional clay.

Name: Lord of the Manor
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point in each scoring category where you have scored the maximum 4 points. (The bonus point is also awarded for 4 fenced stables.)

Name: Maid
Card Text: Once you have built a Clay hut, place 1 food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Mason
Card Text: Once during the game, at any time after your stone house reaches at least 4 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.

Name: Patron
Card Text: In future, whenever you play an occupation, take 2 food before you pay the costs of the occupation.

Name: Pastor
Card Text: If, when you play this card or later in the game, you are the last player to only have 2 rooms in your home, you receive 3 Wood, 2 Clay, 1 Reeds and 1 Stone.

Name: Plough Driver
Card Text: Once you have a stone house, you can pay 1 food at the start of each round to plough (at most) 1 field.

Name: Plough Maker
Card Text: Whenever you use either of the 'Plough' or 'Plough Fields and Sow' Action spaces, you can pay 1 food to plough 1 additional field.

Name: Mushroom Collector
Card Text: Whenever you use a family member's action to take wood from an Action space, you can leave 1 of the wood on the space and take 2 food in exchange.

Name: Braggart
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1/3/5/7/9 Bonus points for having 5/6/7/8/9 improvements in front of you.

Name: Ratcatcher
Card Text: In rounds 10 and 12, all other players may not place 1 of their family's offspring (if they have any). This card may only be played until the end of Round 9.

Name: Renovator
Card Text: Pay 2 less clay to renovate to a clay hut; 2 less stone to renovate to a stone house.

Name: Conservator
Card Text: You can renovate your wooden hut to a stone house without first needing to renovate it to a clay hut.

Name: Cattle Whisperer
Card Text: Add 5 and 9 to the current round. Place 1 cattle on the corresponding Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the cattle.

Name: Seasonal Worker
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Day Labourer' Action space, you receive 1 additional grain. From Round 6, you can choose to take 1 vegetable instead.

Name: Shepherd
Card Text: During each Harvest, if you have at least 4 sheep during the Breeding phase, take 2 lambs instead of 1 as long as you have room for them.

Name: Master Shepherd
Card Text: Place 1 sheep on each of the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the sheep.

Name: Reed Collector
Card Text: Place 1 reeds on each of the next 4 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the reeds.

Name: Swineherd
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Wild boar' Action space, you receive 1 additional wild boar from the supply.

Name: Stable Hand
Card Text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you also receive 1 stable which you must build immediately.

Name: Stablemaster
Card Text: In (at most) one of your unfenced stables, you can hold up to 3 animals of the same type.

Name: Quarryman
Card Text: You can use the Quarryman at any time to convert stone to food. For each stone you convert, take 2 food.

Name: Stone Carrier
Card Text: Whenever you take stone with an action, you can also take 1 additional stone. If you also receive other resources, this costs you 1 food.

Name: Stonecutter
Card Text: All Improvements, rooms and renovations cost 1 stone less.

Name: Dancer
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, you receive at least 4 Food, even if only 1 to 3 Food are on the space.

Name: Stockman
Card Text: Take 1 cattle when you build your second stable, 1 wild boar when you build your third and 1 sheep when you build your fourth.

Name: Potter
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Potter can convert up to 1 clay to 2 food.

Name: Tenant Farmer
Card Text: You immediately receive a loan of one of each type of animal. Before scoring, return the 3 animals. For each animal that you cannot or do not want to return, deduct 1 point.

Name: Animal Keeper
Card Text: You can keep sheep, wild boar and cattle in the same pasture. This applies to all your pastures.

Name: Reeve
Card Text: Take 1/2/3/4 wood if there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play. At the end of the game, players with the most played Occupation cards each receive 3 bonus points.

Name: Carpenter
Card Text: To extend your home, you need only 3 of the appropriate resource and 2 reeds.

Name: Fieldsman
Card Text: Whenever you sow 1 field, place 2 extra goods from the supply on the newly sown field. Whenever you sow 2 fields, place 1 extra good on each.

Name: Well Builder
Card Text: For you, the Well is not a Major but a Minor improvement and costs only 1 stone and 1 wood to build.

Name: Village Elder
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, all players with the most Improvements receive 3 bonus points each.

Name: Social Climber
Card Text: Whenever you are the first player to renovate to a clay hut or a stone house, you receive 3 stone. If you are the second, you receive 2 stone; the third, you receive 1 stone.

Name: Harvest Helper
Card Text: At the start of the Feeding phase in each Harvest, you can take 1 grain from 1 field belonging to another player. S/he takes 2 food from the supply.

Name: Field Worker
Card Text: Whenever another player sows one or more fields, you receive 1 grain in a 3-player game, 1 food in a 4 or 5 player game.

Name: Field Watchman
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 grain' Action space, you can also plough up to 1 field.

Name: Gardener
Card Text: Take vegetables from the supply and not from your vegetable field whenever you harvest them - you keep the vegetables on the fields.

Name: Church Warden
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, any player who performed actions with at least 5 people in Round 14 receives 3 bonus points.

Name: Businessman
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Starting player' Action space, you can play an additional Minor Improvement or a Major Improvement after you play the Minor Improvement.

Name: Sycophant
Card Text: Whenever another player uses the 'Take 1 Grain' Action space, s/he must first pay you 1 food. In addition, you receive 1 food from the supply, even when you take the grain yourself.

Name: Clay Digger
Card Text: Include the '1 Clay' Action card from the 3-player game as an additional clay pit. Immediately place 3 clay on the card and add 1 clay at the start of each round. Any player who uses this action must pay you 3 food.

Name: Manservant
Card Text: When you build a Stone house, place 3 food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Midwife
Card Text: Whenever another player has a larger family than you after having offspring, you receive 1 food. If s/he has at least 2 more family members than you, you receive 2 food.

Name: Farm Steward
Card Text: Once you live in a Clay hut or Stone house, play your next Family growth action as 'Family growth even without room in the hut or house'.

Name: Wood Buyer
Card Text: Whenever a player receives wood from an action, you can buy 1 wood from him/her for 1 food (even without his/her agreement).

Name: Wood Collector
Card Text: Place 1 wood on each of the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the wood.

Name: Hide Farmer
Card Text: At the end of the game, you can pay 1 food each for any number of unused fields. These do not lose you points in the scoring.

Name: Juggler
Card Text: Whenever you use the Travelling Players action on an Action space, you can take twice as much food as is on the card.

Name: Chamberlain
Card Text: From Round 11, you (and only you) can use the Actions on the Round cards for the following rounds.

Name: Corn Profiteer
Card Text: You can convert 1 grain to 3 food at any time with the Corn Profiteer. Any other player can stop this by paying you 2 food for the grain.

Name: Cowherd
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Cattle' Action space, take 1 additional cattle from the supply.

Name: Clay Plasterer
Card Text: Renovating your Wooden hut to a Clay hut costs you only 1 clay and 1 reeds. Each room of your Clay hut costs you 3 clay and 2 reeds.

Name: Clay Hut Builder
Card Text: Once you live in a Clay hut, place 2 clay on each of the next 5 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the clay.

Name: Bricklayer
Card Text: Each improvement and renovation costs you 1 clay less. Each room costs you 2 clay less.

Name: Layabout
Card Text: Once you have played this card, you may not take part in the next Harvest. (You do not need to feed your family during that Harvest.)

Name: Market Crier
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 grain' Action space, you can take an additional 1 grain and 1 vegetables. If you do this, the other players each receive 1 grain from the supply.

Name: Milking Hand
Card Text: In the Field phase of the Harvest, you receive 1/2/3 food for 1/3/5 cows, without having to give up the cows. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for every 2 cows.

Name: Butcher
Card Text: You can change your animals into food at any time. Take 1 food for each sheep, 2 for each wild boar and 3 for each cattle.

Name: Net Fisherman
Card Text: If one of your people uses an Action space that provides reeds, you can take all food markers from the 'Catch fish' space in the Returning home phase.

Name: Puppeteer
Card Text: Whenever another player chooses the 'Travelling players' action on an Action space, you can pay 1 food to play an occupation.

Name: Sheep Whisperer
Card Text: Add 4, 7, 9 and 11 to the current round and place 1 sheep on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the sheep.

Name: Reeds Buyer
Card Text: Whenever reeds are taken for the first time in a round, you may give the player 1 food in exchange for 1 of the reeds. The other player receives an additional 1 food from the supply.

Name: Pig Breeder
Card Text: Your wild boar also breed at the end of Round 12, if there is place for the piglet. When you play this card, take 1 wild boar.

Name: Pig Catcher
Card Text: Whenever you use a person to take wood that is on an Action space, you can leave 2 of the wood on the space and take a wild boar instead.

Name: Groom
Card Text: Once you have a Stone house, you can build 1 stable at a cost of 1 wood at the beginning of each round, without placing a person.

Name: Stone Buyer
Card Text: Whenever another player is the first to take stone in a round, you may give him/her 1 food in exchange for 1 of the stone. S/he receives an additional 1 food from the supply.

Name: Stone Carver
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Stone Carver can convert up to 1 stone to 3 food.

Name: Musician
Card Text: Take 1 grain whenever another player takes the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space.

Name: Cabinetmaker
Card Text: In each Harvest, the Cabinetmaker can convert up to 1 wood to 2 food.

Name: Animal Dealer
Card Text: Whenever you use one of the 'Sheep', 'Wild boar' or 'Cattle' Action spaces, you can pay 1 food to take 1 additional animal of that type.

Name: Taster
Card Text: Whenever another player is the Starting Player, you can pay him/her 1 food at the start of the round and be the first to place a family member.

Name: Outrider
Card Text: Whenever you use the most recent Round card with one of your people, take 1 additional grain.

Name: Water Carrier
Card Text: Once any player has built the Well, place 1 food on the remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Fence Builder
Card Text: When you play this card, place one of your fences on an Action space of your choice. If you use an action on this Action space, you can also build fences as an additional action.

Name: Fencer
Card Text: Whenever another player builds 1 to 4 fences, take 1 wood from the supply. Whenever another player builds 5 or more fences, take 2 wood.

Name: Fence Deliveryman
Card Text: Add 6 and 10 to the current Round. Place 4 of your fences on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 2 food to build all 4 fences immediately.

Name: Serf
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Sow and bake bread' action, take 1 grain before the action. Alternatively, you can exchange 1 grain for 1 vegetables.

Name: Adoptive Parents
Card Text: When you take the 'Family Growth' action, you can pay 1 food and place the offspring on an Action space in the same round. The offspring does not then count as 'newborn'.

Name: Casual Labourer
Card Text: Whenever you receive wood, clay, reeds, stone or grain on an Action space, you can buy one more of the same good for 1 food. An additional vegetables costs 2 food.

Name: Acrobat
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action Space, after all other players have had their turns you may move that person to a free 'Plough' or 'Take 1 Grain' Action space and take the action.

Name: Wet Nurse
Card Text: When building, you may grow your family by up to the number of rooms that you build. This costs 1 food per person.

Name: Educator
Card Text: Whenever another player plays an Occupation card, you can pay 3 food to play one yourself. From your 4th Occupation, this only costs 2 food.

Name: Frame Builder
Card Text: In each renovation, you may replace exactly 1 clay or 1 stone with 1 wood. In each extension, you may replace exactly 2 clay or 2 stone with 1 wood.

Name: Basin Maker
Card Text: For each wild boar that you convert into food, you can place up to 2 wood from your personal supply on this card. You receive 1 bonus point for each wood on this card except the 1st, 4th, 7th and 10th.

Name: Organic Farmer
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each pasture that contains at least 1 animal, but could contain at least 3 more animals.

Name: Lazy Student
Card Text: Whenever you let another player draw one of your Occupation cards for you to play, you receive 3 food before you pay the costs of the card.

Name: Constable
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 Rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, any player who has no negative points receives 5 bonus points.

Name: Manufacturer
Card Text: Once you have a Clay hut or a Stone house, the Cabinetmaker's, Pottery and Basket Weaver's are Minor improvements for you and their cost is reduced by 2 resources of your choice.

Name: Forester
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Sowing' action, you can plant up to a maximum of 3 wood on this card. The wood is treated the same as sown grain and is harvested during the Field phase.

Name: Scholar
Card Text: Once you have a Stone house, at the start of a Round you can always either pay 1 Food to play an Occupation card or play an Improvement card by paying its costs.

Name: Tanner
Card Text: Place wild boar and cattle that you convert into food on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/6 slaughtered wild boar as well as for 2/3/4 cattle.

Name: Travelling Salesman
Card Text: Whenever you select the 'Minor Improvement' action on an Action space, you can play a Major instead of a Minor Improvement. If you select the 'Major or Minor Improvement' action, you can play 2 Minor Improvements.

Name: House Steward
Card Text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, players with the most rooms in their home each receive 3 bonus points.

Name: Wood Deliveryman
Card Text: Place 1 wood on each space for Rounds 8 to 14. At the start of these rounds, take the wood.

Name: Wood Distributor
Card Text: At the start of the Work phase, you can distribute the wood from the '3 Wood' Action space evenly onto the neighbouring Clay, Reeds and Catch fish spaces.

Name: Hand Waterer
Card Text: With the Hand Waterer you can convert clay into food at any time. You receive 1 food per clay. If any player has built a Well, you receive 3 food for every 2 clay.

Name: Smallholder
Card Text: Your pastures that can hold 2 animals can hold 3 animals. While you have at most 2 fields, add 1 extra grain or vegetables when you sow.

Name: Store Clerk
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 5 Stone at the start of a round, take 1 extra Stone. With 6 Reeds, take 1 Reeds, with 7 Clay take 1 Clay and with 8 Wood take 1 Wood.

Name: Storehouse Keeper
Card Text: Whenever you use a person's action to take reeds and stone, you also receive your choice of 1 clay or 1 grain.

Name: Countryman
Card Text: After all players have placed their Family members, you may move one of your Family members from a 'Take 1 Grain' or 'Take 1 Vegetable' Action space to a free 'Sowing' Action space after you have taken the Grain or Vegetables.

Name: Clay Worker
Card Text: Whenever you use an action to take wood or clay, you also receive 1 additional clay.

Name: Lover
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately carry out a 'Family growth even without room in the hut or house' action.

Name: Market Woman
Card Text: Whenever you receive vegetables through a person's action or through a Minor Improvement, take 2 additional grain.

Name: Ploughman
Card Text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the current round and place 1 field on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you can plough that field for 1 food.

Name: Brushwood Collector
Card Text: You may replace the required reeds with 1 wood for any renovation or extension.

Name: Cattle Breeder
Card Text: Your cattle also breed at the end of Round 12, if there is place for the calf. When you play this card, you receive 1 cattle.

Name: Seed Seller
Card Text: Whenever you use the Action space 'Take 1 Grain', take 1 additional grain. When you play this card, take 1 grain.

Name: Sheep Farmer
Card Text: Whenever you take sheep with one of your people, take an additional sheep from the supply. You can exchange 3 sheep for 1 cattle and 1 wild boar at any time.

Name: Shepherd Boy
Card Text: Once you live in a Stone house, place 1 sheep on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the sheep.

Name: Slaughterman
Card Text: Whenever another player converts animals to food, take 1 food from the supply. In the Feeding phase, you are the last player to play.

Name: Schnaps Distiller
Card Text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Schnaps Distiller can convert up to 1 vegetable to 5 food.

Name: Wood Carver
Card Text: Each round, either an Improvement, a room of a Wooden hut, a stable or a fence costs you 1 wood less.

Name: Pig Whisperer
Card Text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the current round and place 1 wild boar on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the wild boar.

Name: Stone Breaker
Card Text: At any time, you can renovate your Clay hut to a Stone house without using the 'Renovation' Action space. (You must still pay the costs of the renovation.)

Name: Veterinarian
Card Text: When you play this card, place 4 sheep, 3 wild boar and 2 cattle in a container. At the start of each round, draw two animals. If they are the same, keep one. Return the 1 or 2 animals to the container.

Name: Animal Handler
Card Text: Place 1 sheep on the space for Round 7, 1 wild boar on Round 10 and 1 cattle on Round 14. At the start of these Rounds, you can buy the animal for 1 food.

Name: Animal Tamer
Card Text: You can keep 1 animal in each room of your home. You may keep more than 1 type of animal in your home.

Name: Animal Breeder
Card Text: When you fence unused spaces to create at least one new pasture, you can buy a pair of animals: 2 sheep for 1 food, 2 wild boar for 2 food or 2 cattle for 3 food.

Name: Foreman
Card Text: At the start of the Work phase, you can place 1 Food from the general supply on an Action space of your choice.

Name: Weaver
Card Text: Whenever you have at least 2 sheep at the start of the Work phase, you receive 1 food.

Name: Resource Seller
Card Text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Stone, Clay, Stone, Clay, Reeds, Clay, Wood on this card. Take the top marker when you receive that type of resource.

Name: Magician
Card Text: Whenever you use your last person to choose the 'Travelling Players' action on an Action space, take an additional 1 grain and 1 food.

Name: Fence Overseer
Card Text: Each Round, for a cost of 1 food, you can immediately fence a pasture of 1 farmyard space around a stable that you have just built. You do not need to pay wood for the fences.

Name: Field
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately plough 1 field. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Fishing Rod
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, you receive 1 additional food. From Round 8, you receive 2 additional food.

Name: Axe
Card Text: Whenever you add a room to your Wooden hut, you only pay 2 wood and 2 reeds.

Name: Baker's Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can use the Baker's Oven to convert up to 2 grain into 5 food each. When you play this card, you can also take the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Baking Tray
Card Text: Clay Ovens and Stone Ovens are Minor improvements for you. Clay, Stone and Wooden ovens cost you 1 resource (of your choice) less.

Name: Building Material
Card Text: Take either 1 wood or 1 clay when you play this card. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Windmill
Card Text: At any time, you can convert grain to 2 food, without having to bake.

Name: Beanfield
Card Text: When Sowing, you can plant vegetables on this card as though it were a field.

Name: Gypsy's Crock
Card Text: Take 1 additional food whenever you convert 2 goods to food at one time using a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth or Cooking Corner.

Name: Simple Fireplace
Card Text: You can exchange goods for food at any time: Vegetables 2 food; Sheep 1 food; Wild boar 2 food; Cattle 3 food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 2 food per grain.

Name: Half-timbered House
Card Text: At the end of the game you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your Stone house. (In total, you receive 3 points instead of 2 per room.)

Name: Raft
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, take an additional 1 food or 1 reeds.

Name: Manger
Card Text: At the end of the game, if your pastures occupy 6/7/8/9+ farmyard spaces, you receive 1/2/3/4 bonus points.

Name: Animal Pen
Card Text: Place 2 food on each remaining Round space. At the start of each round, you receive the food.

Name: Spices
Card Text: Whenever you convert vegetables to food using a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth or Cooking Corner, take 1 additional food.

Name: Plane
Card Text: Whenever you turn 1 wood into food using the Cabinetmaker's, Sawmill or Cabinetmaker, you receive 1 additional food. You can choose instead to turn a second wood into exactly 2 food.

Name: Wooden Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can use the Wooden Oven to turn any number of grain into 3 food each. When you play this card, you can also take the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Clogs
Card Text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for a clay hut, or two for a stone house.
Bonus Points: 1 for clay hut, 2 for stone house

Name: Shepherd's Pipe
Card Text: You can hold up to 2 additional sheep on each of the pastures where you keep sheep. In unfenced stables, you can keep up to 2 sheep.

Name: Canoe
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Catch Fish' Action space, you receive an additional 1 food and 1 reeds.

Name: Carp Pond
Card Text: Place 1 food on each remaining odd-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Potato Dibber
Card Text: Whenever you sow fields with vegetables, place 1 additional vegetable on each newly sown field.

Name: Ceramics
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 2 food. From now, the Pottery is a Minor Improvement for you and costs you nothing.

Name: Basket
Card Text: Whenever you use a person's action to take wood, you can leave 2 of the wood on the Action space and receive 3 food in exchange.

Name: Corn Scoop
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Take 1 grain' Action space, you receive 1 additional grain.

Name: Clay Roof
Card Text: You can replace 1 or 2 reeds with the same amount of clay whenever you extend or renovate your home.

Name: Clay Supports
Card Text: Whenever you add a room to your clay hut, you can pay 2 clay, 1 wood and 1 reeds instead of 5 clay and 2 reeds.

Name: Madonna Statue
Card Text: The Madonna Statue has no effect.

Name: Market Stall
Card Text: When you play this card, you receive 1 vegetable. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Mini Pasture
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately fence one space in your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Millstone
Card Text: Whenever you bake 1 or more loaves of bread, take 2 additional food.

Name: Helpful Neighbours
Card Text: When you play this card, take either 1 stone or 1 reeds. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Fruit Tree
Card Text: Place 1 food on each remaining Round space for rounds 8 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.

Name: Outhouse
Card Text: The Outhouse has no effect. You can only build it if at least one other player has fewer than 2 occupations.

Name: Private Forest
Card Text: Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, take the wood.

Name: Sack Cart
Card Text: Place 1 grain each on the spaces for rounds 5, 8, 11 and 14. At the start of these rounds, take the grain.

Name: Lettuce Patch
Card Text: On this card, you can plant vegetables as you would on a field. Vegetables on this field can be converted to 4 food when harvested.

Name: Reed Pond
Card Text: Place 1 Reeds each on the next 3 remaining Round spaces. At the start of each round, you receive the reeds.

Name: Writing Desk
Card Text: Whenever you use an 'Occupation' Action space, you may play 2 occupations one after another. The second occupation costs you 2 food.

Name: Builder's Trowel
Card Text: You can renovate your Wooden hut to a Clay hut at any time, without using the 'Renovate' action. (You must still pay for the Renovation).

Name: Spindle
Card Text: Whenever you have 3 (5) sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1 (2) additional Food.

Name: Stable
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately build 1 stable. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Butter Churn
Card Text: Whenever you have sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1 food for each third sheep. Whenever you have cattle during the Field phase, you receive 1 food for each second cattle.

Name: Quarry
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Day Labourer' Action space, you receive an additional 3 stone.

Name: Stone House Extension
Card Text: When you play this card, immediately extend your stone house by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Stone Tongs
Card Text: Whenever you use one of the 'Stone' Action spaces, you receive 1 additional stone.

Name: Dovecote
Card Text: Place 1 Food each on the spaces for rounds 10 to 14. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Animal Yard
Card Text: Place up to two animals of your choice on this card. They need not be the same type of animal.

Name: Drinking Trough
Card Text: Each Pasture (with or without a stable) can hold up to 2 more animals.

Name: Cattle Market
Card Text: When you play this card, take 1 cattle. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left.

Name: Vehicular Plough
Card Text: Twice during the game, when you use either the 'Plough Fields' or 'Plough Fields and Sow' action, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

Name: Turnwrest Plough
Card Text: Once during the game, when you use either the 'Plough Fields' or 'Plough Fields and Sow' action, you can plough 3 fields instead of 1.

Name: Well
Card Text: Place 1 food each on the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, take the food.

Name: Fireplace (2 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 2 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 2 Food; Cattle 3 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Fireplace (3 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 2 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 2 Food; Cattle 3 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Cooking Hearth (4 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 3 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 3 Food; Cattle 4 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Cooking Hearth (5 clay)
Card Text: At any time, you may exchange goods for food: Vegetables 3 Food; Sheep 2 Food; Wild boar 3 Food; Cattle 4 Food. Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you receive 3 Food per grain.

Name: Basket Weaver's Workshop
Card Text: In each Harvest, you can use the Basket Weaver's to convert at most 1 reeds to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 reeds.

Name: Clay Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can turn at most 1 grain into 5 food. When you take this card, you can immediately use the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Stone Oven
Card Text: Whenever you use the 'Bake bread' action, you can turn up to 2 grain into 4 food each. When you take this card, you can immediately use the 'Bake bread' action.

Name: Joinery
Card Text: In each Harvest, you can use the Joinery to convert at most 1 wood to 2 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 3/5/7 wood.

Name: Pottery
Card Text: In each Harvest, you can use the Pottery to convert at most 1 clay to 2 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 3/5/7 clay.
